title: NOC 41320 – Educational Counsellors | GTR Immigration
description: Educational Counsellors under NOC 41320 are specialized professionals who guide students in academic planning, career development, and personal growth within Canada’s education system. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role is vital in supporting student success at secondary schools, colleges, and universities. These professionals help students make informed decisions about education pathways, career choices, and personal challenges, while contributing to school-based support systems and community outreach initiatives.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group413 – Professional occupations in social and community servicesMinor Group4132 – Educational and employment counsellorsNOC Code41320

duties: Educational Counsellors perform some or all of the following duties:
Counsel students on course selection and academic planning
Advise on career exploration and vocational pathways
Assist with résumé preparation and interview skills
Consult with teachers, parents, psychologists, and social workers
Administer and interpret aptitude and vocational tests
Develop study skills workshops and academic support sessions
Provide academic information to prospective students
Counsel students on personal and social issues
May teach classes in some institutions
employment_requirements: Master’s degree in:Counseling psychologyEducational psychologyDevelopmental psychologySocial work (usually required)
In Quebec:Membership in L'Ordre des conseillers et conseillères d'orientation required to use protected titles
Membership in L'Ordre des conseillers et conseillères d'orientation required to use protected titles
Teacher certification and teaching experience usually required for school-based counsellors
Post-secondary counsellors may require licensing from provincial regulatory bodies
Registration with provincial psychological or social work regulatory bodies may be required
Foreign-trained professionals must complete credential assessment and provincial recognition where applicable.
salary_prospects: $47,400 – $95,300 per year, depending on province, level of education institution, and years of experience.
Higher salaries are common in Northern territories and senior administrative roles.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 41320 is positive due to:
Increased mental health awareness in schools
Growing need for career transition services
Expansion of co-op and employment programs
Post-secondary institutions continue expanding student services departments.
labour_market_demand: Increased focus on student mental health
Higher demand exists in Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, Quebec, and Northern territories.
Opportunity to support youth and community development
Career advancement into senior student services roles
Counsellors with expertise in mental health, Indigenous education, and career transition programs are particularly valued.
pathways: Licensing and teacher certification may be required before employment.
